uploadLockfileCard.title: Upload Lockfile
uploadLockfileCard.description: Upload your package-lock.json file to analyze dependency impact.
uploadLockfileCard.analyzedSuffix: analyzed. Click or drop to replace it.

dependencyGraphCard.title: Dependency Graph
dependencyGraphCard.description: The graph shows how packages connect and which ones have the strongest structural reach in your project.
dependencyGraphCard.mockModeNote: This live demo uses mock data as the production backend is intentionally disabled to avoid hosting costs.
dependencyGraphCard.mockModeNoteSuffix: Full functionality is available in the
dependencyGraphCard.emptyState: Graph visualization will render here after analysis

riskTableCard.title: Top Structural Impact Packages
riskTableCard.description: Packages with the highest structural impact scores in your graph.
riskTableCard.rankingNote: Packages are ranked by structural impact score, not by vulnerability severity.

headers.riskTooltip: Derived only from dependency graph position and reach. It does not indicate known vulnerabilities.
headers.reasonTooltip: A relative measure of how much structural reach this package has in the dependency graph. Higher does not mean a literal count of affected packages.
headers.vulnerabilitiesTooltip: Number of known security vulnerabilities for this exact package version.
headers.highlightTooltip: Highlight this package and dependents in the graph; reveals structural impact and security context.

riskTableCard.emptyTable: Upload a package-lock.json to populate this table
riskTableCard.scrollToTopAriaLabel: Scroll to top of risk table

aiExplanationPanel.title: Structural Impact & Security Context
aiExplanationPanel.structuralSectionHelper: AI-generated overview based on structural impact score, downstream reach, depth, and known vulnerabilities.
aiExplanationPanel.securitySectionHelper: Vulnerability details are shown below, separate from the AI overview.

whyThisMatters.highCritical: This package is both structurally central and affected by critical vulnerabilities, making it particularly important to review.
whyThisMatters.highNoCritical: This package is structurally central to the project, meaning changes could affect many downstream dependencies, even though no critical security issues are currently known.
whyThisMatters.mediumCritical: This package has moderate structural reach but is affected by critical vulnerabilities that may warrant closer attention.
whyThisMatters.mediumNoCritical: This package has moderate structural reach and no known security vulnerabilities, suggesting relatively balanced concern.
whyThisMatters.lowCritical: This package has limited structural reach, but existing critical vulnerabilities may still warrant attention.
whyThisMatters.lowNoCritical: This package has limited structural reach and no known security vulnerabilities, suggesting lower immediate concern.
